qz_26a199a4dc.topic: Explain solving linear equations and inequalities

questions.question_text: What is the first step in solving a linear equation?
choices: Add the same value to both sides of the equation
Subtract the same value from both sides of the equation
Multiply both sides of the equation by the same value
Divide both sides of the equation by the same value

questions.question_text: What is the purpose of isolating the variable in a linear equation?
choices: To make the equation more complex

questions.question_text: What is the difference between a linear equation and a linear inequality?
choices: A linear equation has a variable on both sides, while a linear inequality has a variable on one side
A linear equation has a variable on one side, while a linear inequality has a variable on both sides
A linear equation has a constant on both sides, while a linear inequality has a constant on one side
A linear equation has a variable on one side, while a linear inequality has a variable on one side

questions.question_text: How do you solve a linear inequality?
choices: By adding or subtracting the same value from both sides
By multiplying or dividing both sides by the same value
By isolating the variable on one side of the inequality
By graphing the inequality on a number line

questions.question_text: What is the concept of 'like terms' in linear equations?
choices: Terms that have the same variable and coefficient
Terms that have the same variable but different coefficients
Terms that have different variables but the same coefficient
Terms that have different variables and coefficients

questions.question_text: How do you simplify a linear equation?
What is the purpose of using inverse operations in linear equations?
How do you check the solution to a linear equation?
choices: By plugging the solution back into the original equation

questions.question_text: What is the concept of 'solving for the variable' in linear equations?
choices: Finding the value of the variable that makes the equation true
Finding the value of the variable that makes the equation false
Finding the value of the variable that makes the inequality true
Finding the value of the variable that makes the inequality false

questions.question_text: How do you graph a linear equation?
choices: By plotting two points on the coordinate plane
By using a ruler and pencil

questions.question_text: What is a variable in mathematics?
choices: A number that can be changed
A symbol or letter used to represent an unknown number

questions.question_text: What is an algebraic expression?
choices: A mathematical sentence that contains a variable
A combination of numbers, letters, and mathematical operations
A geometric shape with a variable
A number that can be changed

questions.question_text: What is a monomial?
choices: A combination of two or more variables
A single variable or a number
A combination of three or more variables
A combination of two or more numbers

questions.question_text: What is the associative property of multiplication?
choices: The property that states a(b+c) = ab + ac
The property that states a(b+c) = ab - ac
The property that states a(b+c) = ab + bc
The property that states a(b+c) = ab - bc

questions.question_text: What is the distributive property of multiplication over addition?
choices: The property that states a(b+c) = ab + ac
The property that states a(b+c) = ab - ac
The property that states a(b+c) = ab + bc
The property that states a(b+c) = ab - bc

What is the distributive property of multiplication over addition for the expression a(b + c + d)?
choices: a(b + c + d) = ab + ac + ad
a(b + c + d) = ab - ac - ad
a(b + c + d) = ab + bc + ad
a(b + c + d) = ab - bc - ad

questions.question_text: What is the distributive property of multiplication over subtraction for the expression a(b - c - d)?
choices: a(b - c - d) = ab + ac + ad
a(b - c - d) = ab - ac + ad
a(b - c - d) = ab + ac - ad
a(b - c - d) = ab - ac - ad

questions.question_text: What is the distributive property of multiplication over addition for the expression (a + b)(c + d)?
choices: (a + b)(c + d) = ac + ad + bc + bd
(a + b)(c + d) = ac - ad - bc + bd
(a + b)(c + d) = ac + ad - bc - bd
(a + b)(c + d) = ac - ad + bc - bd

questions.question_text: What is the distributive property of multiplication over subtraction for the expression (a - b)(c - d)?
choices: (a - b)(c - d) = ac - ad + bc - bd
(a - b)(c - d) = ac + ad - bc + bd
(a - b)(c - d) = ac - ad - bc - bd
(a - b)(c - d) = ac + ad + bc + bd

questions.question_text: What is the distributive property of multiplication over addition for the expression a(b + c + d + e)?

a(b + c + d + e) = ab - bc - ad + ae

questions.question_text: What is the distributive property of multiplication over subtraction for the expression a(b - c - d - e)?
choices: a(b - c - d - e) = ab + ac + ad + ae
a(b - c - d - e) = ab - ac + ad + ae
a(b - c - d - e) = ab + ac - ad - ae
a(b - c - d - e) = ab - ac - ad - ae

questions.question_text: What is a characteristic of an image formed in a plane mirror?
choices: It is erect, not upside down
It is larger than the object

questions.question_text: What type of image is formed by a convex mirror for all object positions?
What is a characteristic of a virtual image?
choices: It can be projected on a screen
It is erect, not upside down
It appears to the eye but doesn't actually exist

questions.question_text: What is a characteristic of an image formed by a concave lens for all object positions?
choices: It is virtual, erect, diminished and located between the principal focus and the optical center

questions.question_text: What is a characteristic of an image formed by a plane mirror?
choices: It is virtual and appears to be as far behind the mirror as it is in front

questions.question_text: What type of image is formed by a concave lens when the object is at infinity?
What is a characteristic of an image formed by a convex lens when the object is between the lens and F?
choices: It is virtual, erect, diminished and located behind the object

questions.question_text: What type of image is formed by a convex lens when the object is at F?
What is a characteristic of an image formed by a concave mirror when the object is between F and C?
choices: It is real and larger than the object
It is virtual and behind the mirror

questions.question_text: What is a characteristic of an image formed by a concave lens when the object is between F and 2F?
choices: It is real, inverted and larger than the object
It is virtual and behind the mirror
